title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The UK government has issued a response to recommendations made by a committee on artificial intelligence and public standards, outlining its plans to address the issues raised. The response is based on the committee's 2020 report, Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, and outlines the government's policy direction on this topic.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This policy document reports on the use of copyrighted materials in the development of AI systems, assessing their potential impact on copyright laws. It aims to examine how the use of copyrighted works in AI creation may affect existing copyright regulations and the rights of creators.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education has a position on the use of generative AI in education that outlines how it plans to regulate and support its use. They aim to ensure that generative AI is used in a way that complements and improves teaching and learning, rather than replacing or undermining it.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport artificial intelligence action plan outlines how the Department for Transport (DfT) will work with the transport sector to harness the benefits and minimize the risks of artificial intelligence. This plan aims to ensure a safe and efficient integration of AI into the transport system.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This document reports on the current state of using copyrighted works in the creation of artificial intelligence systems, focusing on adherence to Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act. It provides an update on the development of AI systems and their reliance on copyrighted material.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to recommendations from the Regulatory Horizons Council to regulate the use of artificial intelligence as a medical device. This policy aims to address the regulation of AI in medical contexts for safe and effective use.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual is developing a plan to regulate how artificial intelligence is used in qualifications, such as exams, to ensure fairness and accuracy. This approach was requested by government ministers who want to oversee the use of AI in the education sector.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report from the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, highlighting the need to address concerns about the development and use of AI in the country. The response aims to address and implement recommendations to ensure the safe and responsible development of AI technology.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document sets out the terms of reference for a review into the intersection of artificial intelligence and public standards. The review aims to clarify the relationship between AI and public standards.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The CSPL submitted evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on how to govern artificial intelligence. The submission aims to provide input on guidelines for regulating AI.
